questions.question: evaporation Which processes involve a change of state? A 1 and 2 B    1 and 3 C    2 and 4 D    3 and 4 2 A  student  measures  the  time  taken  for  2.0 g  of  magnesium  to  dissolve  in  50 cm 3 of  dilute sulfuric acid. Which apparatus is essential to complete the experiment? 1     stop-clock 2     measuring     cylinder 3     thermometer 4     balance, A 1, 2 and 4 B    1 and 2 only C    1 and 4 only D    2, 3 and 4 3 Which method should be used to separate a mixture of two liquids?, A crystallisation, B electrolysis, C filtration, D fractional    distillation 3 4     Lead(II) iodide is insoluble in water. Lead( II) iodide is made by adding aqueous lead(II) nitrate to aqueous potassium iodide. Which  pieces  of  apparatus  are  needed  to  obtain  solid  lead( II)  iodide  from  20 cm 3 of  aqueous lead( II) nitrate? 12345, A 1, 2 and 4 B    1, 3 and 5 C    1, 4 and 5 D    2, 4 and 5

Which apparatus is essential to complete the experiment? 1     stop-clock 2     measuring     cylinder 3     thermometer 4     balance
A 1, 2 and 4 B    1 and 2 only C    1 and 4 only D    2, 3 and 4 3 Which method should be used to separate a mixture of two liquids?

D fractional    distillation 3 4     Lead(II) iodide is insoluble in water. Lead( II) iodide is made by adding aqueous lead(II) nitrate to aqueous potassium iodide. Which  pieces  of  apparatus  are  needed  to  obtain  solid  lead( II)  iodide  from  20 cm 3 of  aqueous lead( II) nitrate? 12345

questions.question: Which row describes the structure of the positive ion in sodium chloride? protons         electrons         neutrons A 11                 11                 12, B 11                 10                 12, C 17                 17                 18, D 17                 18                 18 4

questions.question: Which pair of statements about diamond and graphite is correct? diamondgraphite A Diamond and graphite are both pure carbon. They are both macromolecules., B Diamond and graphite can both be used as electrodes. Graphite is also used as a lubricant., C Diamond has covalent bonds. Graphite has ionic bonds., D Diamond is hard with a high melting point. Graphite is soft with a low melting point.
questions.text: Which pair of statements about diamond and graphite is correct? diamondgraphite A Diamond and graphite are both pure carbon. They are both macromolecules., B Diamond and graphite can both be used as electrodes. Graphite is also used as a lubricant., C Diamond has covalent bonds. Graphite has ionic bonds., D Diamond is hard with a high melting point. Graphite is soft with a low melting point.
options: A Diamond and graphite are both pure carbon. They are both macromolecules.
B Diamond and graphite can both be used as electrodes. Graphite is also used as a lubricant.
C Diamond has covalent bonds. Graphite has ionic bonds.
D Diamond is hard with a high melting point. Graphite is soft with a low melting point.

questions.question: What is the nucleon number of an atom? A the number of neutrons, B the number of protons, C the total number of protons and neutrons, D the total number of protons and electrons
questions.text: What is the nucleon number of an atom? A the number of neutrons, B the number of protons, C the total number of protons and neutrons, D the total number of protons and electrons
options: C the total number of protons and neutrons
D the total number of protons and electrons

questions.question: The relative formula mass, M r , of calcium carbonate, CaCO 3 , is 100. What is the mass of carbon present in 100 g of calcium carbonate? A 12 g B    36 g C    40 g D    60 g 5
questions.text: The relative formula mass, M r , of calcium carbonate, CaCO 3 , is 100. What is the mass of carbon present in 100 g of calcium carbonate? A 12 g B    36 g C    40 g D    60 g 5
options: A 12 g B    36 g C    40 g D    60 g 5

questions.question: Two electrolysis experiments were carried out as shown. The graphite electrodes are labelled 1– 4. power supply molten sodium chloride 1234 concentrated aqueous sodium chloride ++ –– power supply Which row describes the products at the electrodes in these experiments? electrode 1 electrode 2 electrode 3 electrode 4 A chlorine                hydrogen                chlorine                hydrogen, B chlorine                  sodium                  chlorine                  hydrogen, C chlorine                  sodium                  hydrogen                  chlorine, D sodium                  chlorine                  sodium                  chlorine

questions.question: 10 g of ammonium nitrate is added to water at 25 °C and the mixture stirred. The ammonium nitrate dissolves and, after one minute, the temperature of the solution is 10 °C. Which word describes this change? A endothermic, B exothermic, C neutralisation, D reduction
questions.text: 10 g of ammonium nitrate is added to water at 25 °C and the mixture stirred. The ammonium nitrate dissolves and, after one minute, the temperature of the solution is 10 °C. Which word describes this change? A endothermic, B exothermic, C neutralisation, D reduction

questions.text: Which process involves a chemical change? A dissolving    copper( II) sulfate, B distilling    ethanol, C freezing    water, D neutralising    copper( II) oxide 6

questions.question: Lumps of limestone react with dilute hydrochloric acid according to the equation shown. CaCO 3 +  2HCl  →  CaCl 2 +  H 2 O  +  CO 2 Which change in conditions decreases the rate of the reaction? A increase the concentration of the acid, B increase the volume of the acid, C increase the size of the lumps of limestone, D increase the temperature
questions.text: Lumps of limestone react with dilute hydrochloric acid according to the equation shown. CaCO 3 +  2HCl  →  CaCl 2 +  H 2 O  +  CO 2 Which change in conditions decreases the rate of the reaction? A increase the concentration of the acid, B increase the volume of the acid, C increase the size of the lumps of limestone, D increase the temperature
options: A increase the concentration of the acid
B increase the volume of the acid
C increase the size of the lumps of limestone

questions.question: Which reaction is reversible? A Cu  +  ZnSO 4 →  CuSO 4 +  Zn, B CuO  +  H 2 SO 4 →  CuSO 4 +  H 2 O, C CuO  +  H 2 →  Cu  +  H 2 O, D CuSO 4 •5H 2 O  →  CuSO 4 +  5H 2 O
questions.text: Which reaction is reversible? A Cu  +  ZnSO 4 →  CuSO 4 +  Zn, B CuO  +  H 2 SO 4 →  CuSO 4 +  H 2 O, C CuO  +  H 2 →  Cu  +  H 2 O, D CuSO 4 •5H 2 O  →  CuSO 4 +  5H 2 O
options: B CuO  +  H 2 SO 4 →  CuSO 4 +  H 2 O
C CuO  +  H 2 →  Cu  +  H 2 O

questions.question: The reaction between magnesium and carbon dioxide is shown in the equation. 2Mg  +  CO 2 →  2MgO  +  C Which statement describes what happens in this reaction? A Carbon is oxidised., B Magnesium    is    reduced., C Neither oxidation nor reduction happens., D The carbon in carbon dioxide is reduced.
questions.text: The reaction between magnesium and carbon dioxide is shown in the equation. 2Mg  +  CO 2 →  2MgO  +  C Which statement describes what happens in this reaction? A Carbon is oxidised., B Magnesium    is    reduced., C Neither oxidation nor reduction happens., D The carbon in carbon dioxide is reduced.
options: C Neither oxidation nor reduction happens.
D The carbon in carbon dioxide is reduced.

questions.question: Barium hydroxide is an alkali. It reacts with hydrochloric acid. How does the pH of the hydrochloric acid change as an excess of aqueous barium hydroxide is added? A The pH decreases from pH 14 and becomes constant at pH 7., B The pH decreases from pH 14 to about pH 1., C The pH increases from pH 1 and becomes constant at pH 7., D The pH increases from pH 1 to about pH 14. 7
questions.text: Barium hydroxide is an alkali. It reacts with hydrochloric acid. How does the pH of the hydrochloric acid change as an excess of aqueous barium hydroxide is added? A The pH decreases from pH 14 and becomes constant at pH 7., B The pH decreases from pH 14 to about pH 1., C The pH increases from pH 1 and becomes constant at pH 7., D The pH increases from pH 1 to about pH 14. 7
options: A The pH decreases from pH 14 and becomes constant at pH 7.
B The pH decreases from pH 14 to about pH 1.
C The pH increases from pH 1 and becomes constant at pH 7.
D The pH increases from pH 1 to about pH 14. 7

questions.question: Copper(II)  sulfate  crystals  are  blue.  They  are  made  by  adding  an  excess  of  copper(II) oxide  to sulfuric acid. The mixture is heated and stirred. It is then filtered and the filtrate is allowed to evaporate, leaving blue crystals. Why is filtration necessary? A to remove soluble properties, B to remove sulfuric acid, C to remove the blue crystals, D to remove unreacted copper( II) oxide
questions.text: Copper(II)  sulfate  crystals  are  blue.  They  are  made  by  adding  an  excess  of  copper(II) oxide  to sulfuric acid. The mixture is heated and stirred. It is then filtered and the filtrate is allowed to evaporate, leaving blue crystals. Why is filtration necessary? A to remove soluble properties, B to remove sulfuric acid, C to remove the blue crystals, D to remove unreacted copper( II) oxide
options: C to remove the blue crystals
D to remove unreacted copper( II) oxide

questions.question: Astatine is below iodine in Group VII in the Periodic Table. Which row describes the properties of astatine? state at room temperature reactivity A gas displaces chlorine, bromine and iodine, B gas displaces iodine but does not displace chlorine or bromine, C solid displaces iodine but does not displace chlorine or bromine, D solid does not displace chlorine, bromine or iodine
questions.text: Astatine is below iodine in Group VII in the Periodic Table. Which row describes the properties of astatine? state at room temperature reactivity A gas displaces chlorine, bromine and iodine, B gas displaces iodine but does not displace chlorine or bromine, C solid displaces iodine but does not displace chlorine or bromine, D solid does not displace chlorine, bromine or iodine
options: A gas displaces chlorine, bromine and iodine
B gas displaces iodine but does not displace chlorine or bromine
C solid displaces iodine but does not displace chlorine or bromine
D solid does not displace chlorine, bromine or iodine

questions.question: Which statement explains why elements in Group VIII of the Periodic Table are unreactive? A They are monatomic gases., B They form stable diatomic molecules., C They have a full outer shell of electrons., D They share electrons with each other. 9
questions.text: Which statement explains why elements in Group VIII of the Periodic Table are unreactive? A They are monatomic gases., B They form stable diatomic molecules., C They have a full outer shell of electrons., D They share electrons with each other. 9
options: B They form stable diatomic molecules.
C They have a full outer shell of electrons.
D They share electrons with each other. 9

questions.question: A farmer treats a field with calcium hydroxide to make it less acidic. When   the   farmer   adds   ammonium   nitrate   fertiliser   to   the   field   immediately   after   the calcium hydroxide, they react. Why does this reaction make the fertiliser less effective? A It makes ammonia gas, so less nitrogen is absorbed by the soil., B It makes an acid, making the soil acidic again., C It makes nitrogen gas, so less nitrogen is absorbed by the soil., D It makes the fertiliser too strong, stopping the plants growing so well. 11
questions.text: A farmer treats a field with calcium hydroxide to make it less acidic. When   the   farmer   adds   ammonium   nitrate   fertiliser   to   the   field   immediately   after   the calcium hydroxide, they react. Why does this reaction make the fertiliser less effective? A It makes ammonia gas, so less nitrogen is absorbed by the soil., B It makes an acid, making the soil acidic again., C It makes nitrogen gas, so less nitrogen is absorbed by the soil., D It makes the fertiliser too strong, stopping the plants growing so well. 11
options: A It makes ammonia gas, so less nitrogen is absorbed by the soil.
B It makes an acid, making the soil acidic again.
C It makes nitrogen gas, so less nitrogen is absorbed by the soil.
D It makes the fertiliser too strong, stopping the plants growing so well. 11
